title: Load-store queue for block-based processor
abstract: Technology related to load-store queues for block-based processor architectures is disclosed. In one example of the disclosed technology, a processor includes issue logic and a load-store buffer. The issue logic can be configured to issue load and store instructions out of program order. Each of the load and store instructions can include an identifier specifying a relative program order of the respective instruction. The load-store buffer can be configured to enqueue the issued load and store instructions; generate hash values for addresses of the load and store instructions; and update a hash data structure using the generated hash values for the issued store instructions as an index of the hash data structure. For the load instructions, the hash data structure can be searched to generate load response data for the load instructions. The generated load response data can be forwarded to an execution unit of the processor.
claims:
Claims 1. A processor comprising: issue logic configured to issue load and store instructions out of program order, each of the load and store instructions including an identifier specifying a relative program order of the respective instructions; and a load-store buffer configured to: enqueue the issued load and store instructions; generate hash values for addresses of the enqueued load and store instructions; update a hash data structure using the generated hash values for the issued store instructions as an index of the hash data structure; for the load instructions, search the hash data structure and generate load response data for the enqueued, issued load instructions based on the search; and forward the generated load response data to an execution unit of the processor. 2. The processor of claim 1, wherein the hash data structure includes a hash table, and the load response data for the load instructions is generated by searching the hash table for store instructions with a matching hash value of the respective load instructions and matching the address of the respective load instructions. 3. The processor of any one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the load-store buffer is further configured to receive a store mask indicating a relative position in program order of the store instructions within an instruction block, and the store mask is used to determine when to search the hash data structure. 4. The processor of claim 3, wherein the hash data structure includes a hash table, and the search of the hash table for a respective load instruction occurs only after all store instructions earlier in program order have been enqueued. 5. The processor of claim 4, wherein the load-store buffer is further configured to commit the instruction block only after all store instructions indicated in the store mask have been enqueued, and committing the instruction block comprises issuing a store request to a memory subsystem for at least one of the enqueued store instructions. 6. The processor of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the hash data structure includes a Bloom filter data structure and the search of the hash data structure comprises determining whether there is a hit in the Bloom filter data structure corresponding to the generated hash value for a respective load instruction. 7. The processor of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the references to the enqueued store instructions are stored in the hash data structure using a linked-list. 8. The processor of claim 7, wherein the load and store instructions are arranged in instruction blocks, and references to the enqueued store instructions are removed from the linked list of the hash data structure after a commit of the instruction block, the commit of the instruction block comprising issuing at least one store request to a memory subsystem for the enqueued store instructions. 9. A method comprising: with a processor, receiving a plurality of issued load and store instructions for an instruction block, at least one of the issued instructions being issued out of program order; updating a hash data structure indexed with a hash value based on an address of at least one of the issued store instructions; generating load response data by accessing the hash data structure using an index based on an address of a load instruction; and forwarding the load response data to execution logic of the processor for completing the load instruction. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the load response data of the load instruction is generated only after all store instructions earlier in program order of the instruction block have been issued. 1 1. The method of claim 10, further comprising: receiving a store mask identifying the store instructions within the instruction block, and the store mask is used for determining when all store instructions earlier in program order of the instruction block have been issued. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising: committing the instruction block only after all store instructions identified by the store mask have issued. 13. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the store mask is decoded from an instruction header of the instruction block. 14. The method of any one of claims 9-13, wherein generating the load response data comprises merging byte-enabled data from the issued store instructions. 15. A block-based processor compri sing : issue logic configured to issue load and store instructions of an instruction block out of program order, each of the load and store instructions including an address and an identifier specifying a relative program order of the instruction within the instruction 

 block; and a load-store queue comprising: a computer-readable memory configured to store the issued load and store instructions in relative program order using the identifiers specifying the relative program order; hash function logic configured to generate hash values for the addresses of the issued load and store instructions; a hash data structure configured to store information associated with the respective issued store instructions, the information in the hash data structure indexed by the hash values of the addresses of the load and store instructions; and load response logic configured to generate load response data for the load instructions, the load response data for a respective load instruction generated after all issued store instructions earlier in the program order than the respective load instruction have been stored in the computer-readable memory, the load response data generated by searching the hash data structure.
